name: Thin
description: The lightest possible font weight, sometimes called Hairline. Extremely delicate strokes that work best at large display sizes.

name: ExtraLight
description: Also called UltraLight. Very light weight suitable for elegant, refined typographic presentations at medium to large sizes.

name: Light
description: A lighter-than-normal weight that gives text a clean, modern, and airy feel. Readable at body sizes on high-resolution screens.

name: Regular
description: The default font weight for body text across web and print. Balanced stroke width optimised for reading comfort at normal sizes.

name: Medium
description: Slightly heavier than Regular, providing gentle emphasis without the full weight of Bold. Useful for subtle hierarchy within text.

name: SemiBold
description: Also called DemiBold. Noticeably heavier than Regular, adding clear emphasis while remaining less aggressive than Bold.

name: Bold
description: The standard bold weight used for strong emphasis, headings, and drawing attention. The most universally available font weight.

name: ExtraBold
description: Also called UltraBold. A very heavy weight for maximum emphasis in headlines and display contexts. Highly impactful at large sizes.

name: Black
description: Also called Heavy or Ultra. The heaviest weight available, creating maximum visual impact. Best reserved for the largest display text.
